title: RAG Outcomes, Task Framing, Baselines, and Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for rag outcomes, task framing, baselines, and evidence and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for RAG outcome and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Define the user, decision, information need, authoritative sources, freshness need, consequence of error, and observable outcome before choosing RAG.
Specify answerable, unanswerable, ambiguous, unauthorized, stale, and conflicting cases with a useful abstention and escalation contract.
Separate ingestion, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, system, and stakeholder evidence so one passing stage cannot hide another failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rag Task User Decision
steps.instruction:
Order the Rag Task User Decision RAG and Python source-to-claim evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rag Task User Decision.

For RAG and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Rag Task User Decision, keep Rag Task User Decision connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 4135 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate a RAG task on a real decision, owner, evidence contract, and safe abstention; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define the user, decision, information need, authoritative sources, freshness need, consequence of error, and observable outcome before choosing RAG.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Authority Ethics Scope
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Authority Ethics Scope: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 1106 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate a RAG task on a real decision, owner, evidence contract, and safe abstention; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
